display_name: FATE (Federated AI Technology Enabler, WeBank / LF AI)
default_creds: FATEBoard: admin / admin (application.properties + KubeFATE cluster.yaml + FATE issue #2942)
misconfig_patterns: FATEBoard ships admin/admin by default (confirmed application.properties + FATE issue #2942)

FATEFlow auth OFF by default — authentication.client.switch=false, authentication.site.switch=false in service_conf.yaml. CONFIRMED operating in production deployment 150.129.138.81 (FATE 1.11.2, 25-job topology returned unauth via /v1/job/list/job on 2026-06-09).
HMAC-SHA1 site auth is opt-in (Certification doc 1.9.1), not on by default
RollSite / OSX trust = party_id claim in header. No cryptographic binding unless mTLS toggled on. Same model 1.x -> 2.x.
Eggroll clustermanager/nodemanager (4670/4671) — no auth, assumes private subnet (often violated)
/v1/table/info returns schema with PII column names (id_card, mobile, salary, credit_score, hospital encounter features)

active_probe.false_positive_check: FATEFlow returns JSON {"data": {"FATE": "x.x.x"}} — anchor on data.FATE key existence not substring

deployment_tells: Heavy CN deployment expected — Tencent Cloud, Alibaba Cloud, WeBank-affiliated banks
OSX modal port unclear (9883 vs 9370 reuse) — resolve at first verified host
Eggroll cluster nodes 4670/4671 colocated; MySQL backend on adjacent port
KubeFATE manifests on public GitHub provide real-world configs (search github.com/FederatedAI/KubeFATE)
pivot_paths: GET /login + try admin/admin -> FATEBoard full access (default-cred class)
GET /v1/job/list -> full job topology with f_runtime_conf (party host:port pairs, table names) — read-only enumeration
GET /v1/table/info -> data schema with PII column names — finding without payload read
GET /v1/component -> component list; do NOT trigger any /v1/job/submit
vulnerabilities: Zero public CVEs for FederatedAI/FATE or FederatedAI/FATE-Flow in NVD or GitHub Advisory DB — for a 6+ year WeBank-backed framework shipping auth-off defaults this is itself a signal (under-examined / under-disclosed)
use_deserialize_safe_module flag in Flow config 1.7+ — internal awareness of unsafe pickle/PyYAML in pipeline runtime, no public CVE attached
CNVD coverage not exercised this round — direct cnvd.org.cn lookup pending
sources: [CONFIRMED - NuClide 2026-06-09 Cat-53 survey: 4 CN deployments full-stack exposed, 1 with inner-B/outer-1 FATEFlow API verification on FATE 1.11.2]
